findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is missing in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF shows the North Carolina state seal centered at the top of the page; HTML version does not display this seal image

findings.description: The DEQ logo at the bottom of the page is missing in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF shows the DEQ logo near the bottom footer area; HTML version does not display this logo

findings.description: The handwritten signature is missing in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF shows a handwritten signature above 'David Miller, PE'; HTML version only shows the typed name without a signature image

findings.description: Underlined text 'Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference' is rendered with underline styling in HTML, which is acceptable, but worth noting it is preserved
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show underlined text for this sentence

findings.description: The HTML has no h1 or any heading elements at all. The document lacks any heading structure, with all content rendered as plain paragraphs. While this is a letter and doesn't have traditional section headings, there is no document title h1 either.
findings.evidence:
The HTML contains only

tags,

elements, and a
tag but no h1-h6 heading elements anywhere.

findings.description: The HTML contains a nested <main> element inside an outer <main>, which is a structural issue but does not cause content duplication.
findings.evidence: The outer <main> wraps the entire body content, and an inner <main> wraps the letter body. This is invalid HTML structure but doesn't duplicate content.
